articles.title: Understanding Swift Concurrency
articles.author: Jane Appleseed
articles.summary: A practical guide to async/await and actors in Swift 6.
articles.content: Swift concurrency introduces structured concurrency patterns that improve safety and performance across Apple platforms.

articles.title: Clean Architecture on iOS
articles.author: John Architect
articles.summary: Layer separation with MVVM in SwiftUI applications.
articles.content: Clean Architecture keeps business rules independent from frameworks and UI, enabling testable and maintainable codebases.

articles.title: Persisting Favorites with SwiftData
articles.author: Alex Developer
articles.summary: Local persistence patterns for user-curated content.
articles.content: SwiftData integrates with SwiftUI for modern persistence workflows without Core Data boilerplate.

articles.title: Modularizing iOS Apps with SPM
articles.author: Maria Modules
articles.summary: Compile-time boundaries for scalable Swift projects.
articles.content: Swift Package Manager enables feature modules with explicit dependency graphs enforced at build time.

articles.title: Dependency Injection in Swift
articles.author: Chris Container
articles.content: Protocol-oriented constructor injection makes dependencies explicit and improves unit testability.

articles.title: The Repository Pattern Explained
articles.author: Dana Data
articles.summary: Abstracting data sources behind stable domain contracts.
articles.content: Repositories hide whether data comes from JSON, a database, or a remote API from the rest of the application.

articles.title: Modern Testing with Swift Testing
articles.author: Taylor Test
articles.summary: Macro-based tests for domain and presentation layers.
articles.content: Swift Testing provides expressive test definitions that complement XCTest for UI automation.

articles.title: Effective SwiftUI Previews
articles.author: Pat Preview
articles.summary: Preview-driven UI development with mock dependencies.
articles.content: Previews accelerate iteration when ViewModels accept injected use cases and mock repositories.
